title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary of the Commonwealth William Galvin will produce reports on jail voting following a lawsuit resolution.
The VOTES Act, signed in 2022, mandates reports on incarcerated voter participation after each statewide election.
Galvin's office faced criticism for missing reports after 2024 and 2025 elections, citing technical issues for delays.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots, leaving the Buffalo Bills after six years.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye to former NFL MVP Josh Allen, highlighting his potential as a playmaker.
Gilliam aims to enhance the Patriots' running game with his physical blocking, echoing former Patriot James Develin's role.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez underwent shoulder surgery and will miss at least the first two months of the 2026 season.
The Red Sox signed veteran lefty reliever Danny Coulombe to a one-year contract for bullpen depth.
Coulombe posted a 2.30 ERA last season, with stronger performance during his time with the Twins.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, a high school senior, charged with murdering Janet Swallow, 68, in a random attack in Danvers.
Police found DeMayo with a blood-stained knife in Lynn; no known connection between him and the victim.
DeMayo, a Bishop Fenwick High School student, is due in court Friday; investigation ongoing with more details expected.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek public help identifying suspect in assault of pregnant woman at 104 Warren St, Roxbury.
Incident occurred March 1 after payment card dispute at laundromat; suspect kicked victim, fled toward Moreland Street.
Suspect described as 6’0” light-skinned male; police seek woman seen at scene for potential association.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer with flashing blue lights.
Beltran was a former Lawrence officer, fired in March 2025 for setting off a gun on duty and other allegations.
He is suing Lawrence police for an alleged unconstitutional search of his phone leading to his firing.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Eagles reportedly demand a 2027 first-round and 2026 second-round pick for trading A.J. Brown.
NFL insider Mike Giardi believes the Eagles may lower their asking price for A.J. Brown to the Patriots.
Trading A.J. Brown after June 1 could save the Eagles over $26 million in dead cap money.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough closed westbound lanes Thursday morning.
Fire crews from Westborough and Grafton extinguished the fire by 8:30 a.m. with no reported injuries.
All westbound lanes reopened by 1:30 p.m. as officials investigate the cause of the fire.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signed a four-year deal with the New England Patriots worth up to $80 million.
Doubs chose the Patriots to work with quarterback Drake Maye and praised the team's winning culture.
Doubs had 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns with the Green Bay Packers in 2025.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner required a complete engine replacement at 60,000 miles due to a loss of power.
Engine issues in Hyundai/Kia models are linked to poorly machined bearings causing knock and potential engine failure.
Similar engine failure recalls affect Toyota's Tundra/Lexus and GM's trucks/SUVs due to manufacturing defects.
